import io

MAX_POOL_SIZE = 10
MAX_BUFFER_SIZE = 1 << 20


class BufferPool:
    def __init__(self, max_buffers: int = MAX_POOL_SIZE, max_buf_size: int = MAX_BUFFER_SIZE):
        self._buffers: list[bytearray] = []
        self._max_buffers = max_buffers
        self._max_buf_size = max_buf_size

    def acquire(self, size_hint: int = 8192) -> bytearray:
        size_hint = min(size_hint, self._max_buf_size)
        if self._buffers:
            buf = self._buffers.pop()
            if len(buf) < size_hint:
                buf.extend(bytearray(size_hint - len(buf)))
            elif len(buf) > self._max_buf_size:
                buf = bytearray(size_hint)
            return buf
        return bytearray(size_hint)

    def release(self, buf: bytearray) -> None:
        if len(buf) > self._max_buf_size:
            return
        if len(self._buffers) < self._max_buffers:
            self._buffers.append(buf)


pool = BufferPool()


def process_chunk(data: bytes) -> bytes:
    buf = pool.acquire(len(data))
    buf[:len(data)] = data
    result = bytes(buf[:len(data)])
    pool.release(buf)
    return result
